text.title: Consistency and Standards Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Consistency and Standards application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The audit MUST cover all five layers: internal, external, visual, functional, verbal. Partial-layer audits are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-layer audits miss the orthogonal failure modes; visual-only audits ignore terminology drift.

rule.statement: Every audited element MUST carry a numeric variation count. "Many" / "lots" is rejected.
rule.rationale: Numeric counts make the gap measurable and the consolidation outcome verifiable.

rule.statement: Consolidation recommendations MUST be ranked by user impact (frequency × visibility × confusion risk). Unranked lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: Unranked lists devolve to "low-hanging fruit first" and miss the high-impact buried-but-rare cases.

rule.statement: External-layer findings MUST cite the platform convention (iOS HIG section, Material spec, WCAG criterion) the product violates.
rule.rationale: Citing the convention makes the recommendation reviewable; uncited claims become opinion.

rule.statement: Every Must-fix finding MUST be paired with a design-system entry (token, component, or pattern). Findings without codification are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without codification the same drift recurs next quarter; the system entry is the durable fix.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
